questions.title: Class Declaration and Object Creation
questions.description: Create a simple class named 'Student' with a public integer member variable 'rollNumber'. Create an object of this class, assign a value to rollNumber, and print it.
questions.inputFormat: A single integer representing the roll number.
questions.explanation: Define a class with a public member variable. Create an object, assign the input value to the member, and output it.
hints: Create a class with public member
Use dot operator to access member

questions.title: Class with Member Function
questions.description: Create a class 'Calculator' with a public member function 'add' that takes two integers and returns their sum. Use this function to add two given numbers.
questions.outputFormat: Print the sum of the two numbers.
questions.explanation: Define a class with a member function. Create an object and call the member function to perform addition.
hints: Define a member function inside class

questions.title: Constructor Initialization
questions.description: Create a class 'Rectangle' with private member variables length and width. Implement a constructor that initializes these values. Add a public method 'getArea' that returns the area. Create an object using the constructor and print the area.
questions.inputFormat: Two space-separated integers representing length and width.
questions.outputFormat: Print the area of the rectangle.
questions.explanation: Use a constructor to initialize private members. Implement a getter method to calculate and return the area.
hints: Constructor has same name as class
Use this-> or direct assignment in constructor

questions.title: Static Member Variable
questions.description: Create a class 'Counter' with a static integer member variable 'count' and a constructor that increments count each time an object is created. Also add a static method 'getCount' that returns the current count. Create N objects and print the final count.
questions.inputFormat: A single integer N representing number of objects to create.
questions.outputFormat: Print the final count after creating N objects.
questions.explanation: Static members are shared across all objects. Each constructor call increments the static count. Use static method to access it.
hints: Static variable must be defined outside class
Static members are shared across all instances

questions.title: Inheritance and Method Overriding
questions.description: Create a base class 'Animal' with a virtual method 'makeSound' that prints 'Animal sound'. Create a derived class 'Dog' that inherits from Animal and overrides makeSound to print 'Bark'. Create another derived class 'Cat' that overrides makeSound to print 'Meow'. Based on input string (dog or cat), create appropriate object and call makeSound.
questions.outputFormat: Print the sound made by the specified animal.
questions.explanation: Use inheritance to create derived classes. Use virtual functions for runtime polymorphism. Create object based on input type.
hints: Use virtual keyword for base class method
Use override keyword in derived class
Use base class pointer for polymorphism
Remember to delete dynamically allocated memory
